summary: All three attempts failed to resolve two persistent SAST findings: bandit:B301 (unsafe pickle deserialization) and bandit:B608 (SQL injection via string interpolation of filter_clause). The code still uses pickle.loads on user-supplied data and directly interpolates filter_clause into the SQL query, despite the scenario explicitly requiring safe deserialization and parameterized queries. The fixer loop did not correct these issues.
suggested_actions: Replace pickle.loads with a safe deserialization mechanism (e.g., json.loads or an allowlist-based unpickler) to address CWE-502.

Remove the filter_clause from the SQL query string entirely; if dynamic filtering is required, use only parameterized placeholders or validate/sanitize the clause against a whitelist of allowed patterns.
Ensure that the fixer loop actually modifies the code to eliminate the flagged patterns rather than re-outputting similar code.

evidence.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

10         metadata = pickle.loads(b64decode(request["metadata_blob"]))
11     except Exception as e:
